pr.title: feat(temporal): implement Phase 3 Temporal message passing idempotency

comments.body:
## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request significantly enhances the robustness of the MoonMind system by implementing Phase 3 of Temporal message passing idempotency. The changes introduce mechanisms to prevent duplicate processing of commands and ensure that critical activities, especially those with external side effects, execute exactly once, even in the face of retries or network issues. This improves the reliability and predictability of workflow executions. ### Highlights * **Idempotency Documentation**: A new documentation file has been added, detailing Phase 3 of the Temporal Workflow Message Passing Improvements. This document outlines conventions for update IDs, deduplication strategies, idempotency keys at activity boundaries, and the use of Signal-With-Start for logical job guarantees. * **Activity Idempotency Key Implementation**: The core `MoonMind.Run` workflow has been updated to generate and pass `idempotency_key` arguments to several critical activities, including `plan.generate`, `mm.skill.execute`, `proposal.generate`, and `proposal.submit`. This ensures that these operations can be safely retried without unintended side effects. * **Integration Test Coverage**: Integration tests for the `MoonMind.Run` workflow have been enhanced. Mock activity definitions for `plan.generate` and `mm.skill.execute` now include assertions to verify the presence and correct format of the `idempotency_key` in their input arguments, ensuring backward compatibility and proper implementation. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Remove unsupported kwarg from skill activity payload**

Adding `idempotency_key` to the `mm.skill.execute` request breaks the default Temporal skill handler path: `_build_activity_wrapper` in `moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py` expands mapping payloads as `func(self, **request)`, but `TemporalSkillActivities.mm_skill_execute` does not accept an `idempotency_key` parameter. For any run that executes a `tool.type == "skill"` node with the standard worker bindings, this now raises `TypeError: ... unexpected keyword argument 'idempotency_key'` before dispatch, causing the execution stage to fail.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `3247dc48f8`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

## Code Review

This pull request introduces idempotency keys for several Temporal activities (`plan.generate`, `mm.skill.execute`, `proposal_generate`, `proposal_submit`) within the `MoonMind` workflow to enhance deduplication and side-effect handling. A new documentation file (`docs/tmp/015-TemporalIdempotency.md`) outlines the overall strategy for Temporal message passing idempotency. Integration tests were updated to include checks for these new `idempotency_key` arguments. Feedback suggests updating the documentation's example format for idempotency keys to accurately reflect the implementation's use of `workflow_id` and specific identifiers. Additionally, the integration tests for `mock_plan_generate` and `mock_skill_execute` could be made more robust by asserting the exact `idempotency_key` value using available `workflow_id` and `node_id` context, rather than just checking for suffixes.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The example format for the idempotency key is inconsistent with the implementation. The documentation suggests using `workflow.info().run_id`, but the code implementation correctly uses `workflow.info().workflow_id` to ensure idempotency across `Continue-As-New` boundaries. Using `run_id` would limit idempotency to a single workflow run.

Additionally, the example format `{workflow.info().run_id}_step_{step_count}_{activity_name}` is more generic than the implementation, which uses more specific and robust identifiers like `{node_id}` or fixed suffixes for different stages (e.g., `_plan_generate`, `_execute`).

To ensure the documentation accurately reflects the implementation, please update the example.

```suggestion
   - Example format: `{workflow.info().workflow_id}_{stage_or_node_id}_{action}` (e.g., `my-workflow-123_plan_generate` or `my-workflow-123_step-1_execute`)
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The assertion for the `idempotency_key` can be made more specific. The current check only verifies the presence of the `_plan_generate` suffix. Since the `workflow_id` is available in the activity arguments via `execution_ref`, you can construct the exact expected idempotency key and assert against it. This will make the test more robust.

```suggestion
    if "idempotency_key" in args:
        workflow_id = args.get("execution_ref", {}).get("workflow_id")
        if workflow_id:
            assert args["idempotency_key"] == f"{workflow_id}_plan_generate"
        else:
            # Fallback for tests that may not provide full execution_ref
            assert "_plan_generate" in args["idempotency_key"]
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The assertion for the `idempotency_key` can be strengthened. Currently, it only checks for the `_execute` suffix. The `workflow_id` and `node_id`, which are used to construct the key, are available in the activity's `context` argument. You can use these to assert the exact value of the idempotency key, making the test more precise and reliable.

```python
    if "idempotency_key" in args:
        context = args.get("context", {})
        workflow_id = context.get("workflow_id")
        node_id = context.get("node_id")
        if workflow_id and node_id:
            assert args["idempotency_key"] == f"{workflow_id}_{node_id}_execute"
        else:
            # Fallback for tests that may not provide full context
            assert "_execute" in args["idempotency_key"]
```

Adding `idempotency_key` to the `proposal.generate` activity request changes the workflow's recorded activity input payload. This is a replay-breaking change for any existing executions that already have `proposal.generate` scheduled in history. Gate the new field behind a replay-stable `workflow.patched(...)` so old histories keep the original payload shape.

Adding `idempotency_key` to the `proposal.submit` activity request changes the recorded activity input for this workflow and can cause non-determinism when replaying in-flight histories started before the change. Gate this field behind a replay-stable `workflow.patched(...)` so legacy histories continue to match the previously recorded payload.

The documented example idempotency key format uses `workflow.info().run_id` and `step_count`, but the implementation in `MoonMind.Run` currently uses `workflow_id`-based keys (e.g., `{workflow_id}_plan_generate`, `{workflow_id}_{node_id}_execute`). Please update the example (or explicitly document the chosen format and why) so the doc matches the actual convention being used.
```suggestion
   - Example format (current convention): use `workflow.info().workflow_id`, e.g. `{workflow_id}_plan_generate` or `{workflow_id}_{node_id}_execute`. This keeps the key stable across retries and Continue-As-New runs for the same logical job.
```

This section claims Phase 3 tests “explicitly check for the optional presence of `idempotency_key` to guarantee backwards compatibility,” but backwards compatibility for in-flight Temporal workflows depends on replay-identical workflow commands (typically via `workflow.patched(...)` gating), not just activity-side acceptance of missing fields. Consider clarifying this to distinguish payload-schema tolerance from Temporal replay compatibility, or note the required `workflow.patched` strategy.
```suggestion
Per MoonMind's testing policy, workflow-boundary tests must cover compatibility paths for prior payload shapes where in-flight runs may exist. Tests added in Phase 3 explicitly verify that activities tolerate the optional presence or absence of `idempotency_key` in their inputs (payload-schema backwards compatibility), and our Temporal workflows gate any new commands behind `workflow.patched(...)` (or an equivalent pattern) so that in-flight workflows replay with identical command sequences (Temporal replay backwards compatibility).
```

## Pull request overview Implements Phase 3 of Temporal message-passing idempotency by introducing deterministic `idempotency_key` fields on selected `MoonMind.Run` activity payloads, along with integration-test assertions and a supporting design note. **Changes:** - Add `idempotency_key` fields to `plan.generate`, skill execution, and proposal activities invoked by `MoonMind.Run`. - Update integration workflow test mocks to validate `idempotency_key` presence/shape (while tolerating legacy absence). - Add a Phase 3 idempotency/deduplication write-up under `docs/tmp`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tests/integration/workflows/temporal/workflows/test_run.py | Adds assertions on mocked activities to validate `idempotency_key` shape when present. | | moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/run.py | Adds `idempotency_key` fields to multiple `execute_activity` payloads in planning/execution/proposals stages. | | docs/tmp/015-TemporalIdempotency.md | Documents Phase 3 idempotency/deduplication conventions and testing expectations. |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2) **moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/run.py:637** * Adding `idempotency_key` to the `mm.skill.execute`/`mm.tool.execute` activity payload changes the recorded activity input for this workflow. This will break replay for in-flight runs created before this change (Temporal non-determinism). Use a replay-stable `workflow.patched(...)` gate so older histories schedule the activity with the legacy payload and newer runs include the idempotency key. ``` execution_result = await workflow.execute_activity( route.activity_type, { "invocation_payload": invocation_payload, "principal": self._principal(), "registry_snapshot_ref": registry_snapshot_ref, "idempotency_key": f"{workflow.info().workflow_id}_{node_id}_execute", "context": { "workflow_id": workflow.info().workflow_id, "run_id": workflow.info().run_id, "node_id": node_id, }, }, ``` **moonmind/workflows/temporal/workflows/run.py:446** * Adding `idempotency_key` to the `plan.generate` activity payload changes the activity command attributes recorded in workflow history. Any in-flight `MoonMind.Run` executions started before this change will likely hit Temporal non-determinism on replay because the activity input no longer matches the recorded history. Gate the new field behind a replay-stable `workflow.patched(...)` (old path: omit the key; new path: include it) per docs/Temporal/WorkerDeployment.md. ``` plan_result = await workflow.execute_activity( "plan.generate", { "principal": self._principal(), "inputs_ref": input_ref, "parameters": parameters, "idempotency_key": f"{workflow.info().workflow_id}_plan_generate", "execution_ref": { "namespace": workflow.info().namespace, "workflow_id": workflow.info().workflow_id, "run_id": workflow.info().run_id, "link_type": "plan", }, }, ```
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Unused import Import of 'JulesIntegrationStartRequest' is not used. --- To fix an unused import, remove the specific import statement (or the unused name from a multi-name import) so that only actually-used symbols are imported. This keeps the module clean, avoids confusion, and can slightly reduce import overhead.

In this file, the single best fix is to delete the line:

from moonmind.schemas.jules_models import JulesIntegrationStartRequest

and leave all surrounding imports unchanged. This does not affect existing functionality because JulesIntegrationStartRequest is not referenced anywhere in the shown code. The change is localized to moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_runtime.py at line 24; no additional methods, imports, or definitions are required.
